Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the normative description says the process must start with Receive order Then it branches based on inventory check If inventory sufficient normal flow is Receive Check Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory insufficient cancellation flow is Receive Check Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints mentioned Authorize payment must come after Check inventory and before Package items Package items must come after Authorize payment and before Ship order Ship order must come after Package items and never after cancellation Close order must be the final activity in all cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This perfectly matches the normal flow when inventory is sufficient All activities in correct order Close is last No violations here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Package items happens before Authorize payment Thats a direct violation of the constraint that Package must come after payment authorization

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Case 2 violates the rule that Package must follow Authorize payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close After Cancel order which should end the process theyre doing Ship order and Close But the normative description says Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Ship order after Cancel is explicitly forbidden Also Close should be the final activity after Cancel in cancellation scenarios but here Ship happens after Cancel and before Close Two violations shipping after cancellation and ship occurring after cancel when it shouldnt

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close This matches the cancellation flow perfectly Receive Check Cancel Close All in correct order Close is final No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Missing Package items The normative flow requires Package items after Authorize and before Ship Here Ship happens directly after Authorize

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Ship before Package is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now to summarize violations Case 2 Package before Authorize payment Case 3 Ship after Cancel and also Ship after Cancel when Close should follow Cancel directly Case 5 Ship before Package Case 1 and 4 are clean Case 2 3 5 have issues
